top_findings.summary: The skill requests unrestricted bash command execution via `Bash(cmd:*)`, which allows any command to be run on the user's system. While troubleshooting Cursor indexing issues may require specific commands (e.g., `du`, `rm`, `ls` for cache management), granting blanket permission to execute any bash command is excessive and poses a security risk. This violates the principle of data minimization, as a more restricted set of commands (e.g., limited to file operations and process checks relevant to Cursor) would suffice for the stated purpose of resolving indexing problems.
